authoritative_sources.title: AWS Well-Architected Framework
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official architecture framework for AWS workload design quality and tradeoff language across operational excellence, security, reliability, performance, cost, and sustainability.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: scope intake, option framing, tradeoff analysis, architecture decision output

authoritative_sources.title: AWS Well-Architected Framework: Operational Excellence Pillar
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official operations guidance for runbooks, metrics, learning, change, and operational readiness in AWS architectures.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: operability design, monitoring plan, runbook output, change validation

authoritative_sources.title: AWS Well-Architected Framework: Reliability Pillar
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official reliability guidance for failure domains, quota management, backup, disaster recovery, resiliency testing, and recovery objectives.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: resilience review, DR planning, failure-mode analysis, backup and recovery evidence

authoritative_sources.title: AWS Control Tower User Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official guidance for landing zones, account vending, organizational units, controls, and governed multi-account AWS environments.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: landing zone intake, account topology, guardrail review, shared-services architecture

authoritative_sources.title: AWS Organizations User Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official documentation for accounts, organizational units, service control policies, delegated administration, and management-account authority boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: account and governance records, management-account boundary handling, SCP and delegation caveats

authoritative_sources.title: Amazon VPC User Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

authoritative_sources.title: AWS Transit Gateway User Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official documentation for centralized VPC and on-premises connectivity, Transit Gateway attachments, and route table behavior.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: hub-and-spoke design, hybrid connectivity, blast-radius and route-domain review

authoritative_sources.title: AWS Prescriptive Guidance: Migration strategy
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official migration workflow guidance for strategy, assessment, mobilization, migration sequencing, and modernization planning.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: migration intake, dependency analysis, sequencing, cutover validation, rollback planning

authoritative_sources.title: Amazon CloudWatch User Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official observability documentation for metrics, logs, alarms, dashboards, events, and operational telemetry used as runbook evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: AWS Backup Developer Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official documentation for backup plans, vaults, recovery points, restore behavior, copy behavior, and recovery evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: backup and recovery planning, DR validation, restore evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: AWS CloudFormation User Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official IaC documentation for stacks, templates, change sets, drift detection, rollback, and deployment event evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: Terraform AWS Provider Documentation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HashiCorp official AWS provider reference for Terraform-managed AWS resources, provider behavior, and supported resource semantics.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 500-292: NIST Cloud Computing Reference Architecture
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST official cross-industry cloud reference architecture defines cloud actors, activities, roles, and responsibility vocabulary for cloud architecture boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: cross-industry cloud terminology, role-boundary handling, provider-consumer-auditor separation

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST official control catalog gives recognized evidence, monitoring, contingency, configuration, audit, access, and risk terminology for cross-industry cloud evidence handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: control and evidence prerequisite review, caveat handling, conflict escalation

authoritative_sources.title: CIS Amazon Web Services Foundations Benchmark
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark is a recognized secure-configuration benchmark for AWS account and baseline service controls, useful as evidence context for guardrail discussions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: guardrail evidence review, security-boundary handoff, configuration baseline caveats

authoritative_sources.title: Current aws-architecture-specialist package baseline
authoritative_sources.source_class: Current package task baseline from aws-architecture-specialist
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The committed legacy package is authoritative only for preserving current supported tasks, input and output shapes, validation threshold, refusal rules, and no-regression context.

workflow_stages.name: scope, authority, and AWS context intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm AWS scope, request intent, desired output, workload owner, business outcome, account boundary, region, tenancy model, data classification, source precedence, and adjacent-lane indicators before producing architecture guidance.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: tenant evidence and system-of-record assembly
workflow_stages.description: Collect AWS Organizations, Control Tower, VPC, Transit Gateway, workload, IAM touchpoint, backup, monitoring, cost, IaC, change, incident, ADR, diagram, policy, and exception records from tenant-approved systems.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: source selection and precedence review
workflow_stages.description: Choose current AWS, HashiCorp, NIST, CIS, and tenant sources; record freshness, source scope, conflicts, and whether tenant or contractual policy overrides public baseline guidance.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: architecture option and tradeoff analysis
workflow_stages.description: Evaluate account, network, workload placement, resilience, observability, migration, cost, isolation, quota, data residency, and IaC options against stated constraints and failure modes.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: implementation, migration, and operability sequencing
workflow_stages.description: Prepare dependency order, implementation notes, validation checkpoints, rollback moments, observability and backup requirements, DR tests, and owner handoffs without executing changes.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: artifact delivery, refusal, or orchestrator return
workflow_stages.description: Deliver an evidence-linked architecture recommendation, migration plan, operability guide, IaC review, or explicit refusal and escalation note with confidence state and unresolved assumptions.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: VPC, Transit Gateway, endpoint, routing, and isolation topology recommendation
operability, monitoring, backup, incident, and DR runbook guidance
cost, reliability, isolation, and quota tradeoff matrix
CloudFormation, CDK, or Terraform IaC review notes
boundary refusal, escalation, or adjacent-lane handoff note
systems_of_record: AWS account inventory, OU structure, management account, delegated administrator, SCP, and control configuration
AWS VPC, Transit Gateway, Direct Connect, VPN, Route 53, endpoint, subnet, route table, gateway, firewall, and security group inventories
AWS Config, CloudTrail, CloudWatch, AWS Backup, AWS Resilience Hub, Cost Explorer, AWS Budgets, Trusted Advisor, Compute Optimizer, and Well-Architected Tool records
CloudFormation stack events, CDK synth outputs, Terraform state, IaC repositories, CI/CD logs, and change-management tickets
Architecture diagrams, ADRs, CMDB records, workload dependency inventories, incident tickets, runbooks, backup and restore evidence, exception registers, and tenant policies

allowed: Recommend AWS account, landing-zone, network, workload, resilience, migration, operations, cost-performance, and IaC architecture options.
Prepare implementation guidance, validation checkpoints, rollback moments, evidence requests, and owner handoffs.
Identify architecture risks, tradeoffs, assumptions, and domain failure modes without approving execution or accepting risk.
escalate: Required AWS tenant facts, evidence, source freshness, source precedence, data classification, RTO/RPO, SLO, region, residency, or owner authority is missing.
AWS documentation, tenant policy, contractual requirements, customer commitments, regulatory obligations, or reviewer instructions conflict.
The request needs architecture review board, change advisory board, security, identity, network, FinOps, legal, compliance, procurement, or executive approval.
refuse: Application-only engineering implementation, security investigation, enterprise identity policy ownership, general financial forecasting, legal advice, procurement approval, compliance attestation, production execution, or risk acceptance.
Requests to execute infrastructure changes, bypass change control, deploy IaC, perform failover, alter accounts, override guardrails, or approve spend.
Requests to treat generic cloud advice as AWS architecture without AWS-specific records, artifacts, systems, and decision boundaries.

domain_failure_modes: Generic cloud guidance ignores AWS account, region, Availability Zone, service quota, data residency, or service-limit constraints.
Landing-zone recommendation collapses account, OU, management-account, delegated-admin, and guardrail authority boundaries.
Network topology advice omits route table, endpoint, DNS, inspection, hybrid connectivity, or blast-radius consequences.
Reliability design recommends RTO/RPO, DR, or backup patterns without tenant objectives, restore evidence, or failure-domain analysis.
Migration sequencing ignores dependencies, data movement, cutover validation, rollback moments, or change windows.
IaC review overlooks Terraform state, CloudFormation rollback, CDK synthesis, drift detection, policy guardrails, or blast radius.
Cost guidance becomes financial approval instead of technical cost-performance evidence.
Security, identity, network operations, Kubernetes operations, legal, compliance, or production execution work is absorbed instead of routed.
Public guidance and tenant policy conflict without precedence resolution.
Cross-industry evidence appears complete but lacks named AWS systems of record or owner authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing

required_workflow_questions.question: What named-industry records and systems actually govern the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer: AWS Organizations, Control Tower, VPC, Transit Gateway, workload dependency inventories, AWS Config, CloudTrail, CloudWatch, AWS Backup, cost tools, IaC repositories, Terraform state, CloudFormation stack events, ADRs, diagrams, runbooks, change records, policies, exception registers, and owner approval records govern the lane.

required_workflow_questions.question: What failure modes make this industry materially different from a generic function?
required_workflow_questions.answer: AWS architecture fails materially when account hierarchy, region/AZ, quota, route, endpoint, backup, drift, service availability, shared responsibility, and owner authority details are absent; generic cloud advice can create wrong blast radius, cost, resilience, or compliance assumptions.

required_workflow_questions.question: What adjacent authority boundaries constrain the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer: Application engineering, cloud security, incident response, identity architecture, network operations, Kubernetes platform operations, FinOps approval, procurement, legal, compliance attestation, production execution, risk acceptance, and architecture approval boards constrain the lane.

unresolved_ambiguities.notes: No blocking ambiguity remains for the spec pack. At runtime, conflicting AWS guidance, tenant policy, contractual obligations, regulatory requirements, or reviewer instructions require precedence resolution before deterministic recommendations.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: AWS service availability, quotas, pricing, and preview features
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: No blocking ambiguity remains for the spec pack. Runtime materialization must refresh AWS service availability, region support, quotas, pricing, and preview-feature status when those facts affect the answer.
This package remains specification-based. Retaining a trusted generated implementation requires separate frequent-use evidence and approval.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated spec-pack release because tenant-specific uncertainty is represented as runtime prerequisites, refusal triggers, or orchestrator return conditions.

The source set covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling using AWS official documentation, HashiCorp official provider documentation, NIST public standards, CIS benchmark guidance, and the current package baseline only for regression preservation.
